p1.about: Lemuriano is a composer, poet and singer. His shows are crafted as immersive experiences, in which the songs connect and guide the audience through a narrative from beginning to end.
p2.about: In a voice and guitar format, he presents original songs, classics of Brazilian music and poetry, combining a sensitive interpretation with striking arrangements.
carousel.slide1.title: Soberano Jazz Club
carousel.slide1.desc: One of the main music venues in the interior of Rio de Janeiro, where Lemuriano performed two sold-out shows – voice and guitar, and also with a band. In doing so, he consolidated his presence in the region’s original music scene with performances marked by a strong connection with the local audience.
carousel.slide2.title: Show with Hermínio Bello
carousel.slide2.desc: Lemuriano’s show featured a special appearance by Hermínio Bello de Carvalho, in a remarkable moment to the sound of the song 'Dia Sim, Dia Não', composed by both musicians.
carousel.slide3.title: Solar de Botafogo Show
carousel.slide3.desc: Lemuriano’s first show at Solar de Botafogo, in Rio de Janeiro, in 2023, marking another magical moment in the city with an original performance.
carousel.slide4.title: Brigitte Blair Theatre Show
carousel.slide4.desc: An intimate and magical performance that reinforced Lemuriano’s artistic identity and connection with the audience.
carousel.slide5.title: Cesgranrio Show
carousel.slide5.desc: Lemuriano’s first show at Cesgranrio marked his debut at this cultural venue, presenting the audience with an original repertoire that consolidated his presence on new stages.
carousel.slide6.title: Cesgranrio Show 2
carousel.slide6.desc: The second show at Cesgranrio featured a special appearance by Tuca Mei, strengthening the artistic dialogue and expanding the audience’s experience through the meeting of voices and journeys.
carousel.slide7.title: Voice and Guitar Show in Minas Gerais
carousel.slide7.desc: Show performed for the inauguration of the railway station in the city of São Sebastião do Rio Verde, in Minas Gerais.
carousel.slide8.title: Carioca Music Centre
carousel.slide8.desc: Show at the Carioca Music Centre, with a special appearance by Tuca Mei, marking a special moment in Lemuriano’s journey.
carousel.slide9.title: Sesc Rosinha de Valença
carousel.slide9.desc: Show at Sesc Rosinha de Valença, marking Lemuriano’s debut at a Sesc unit, with a full band on stage.
carousel.slide10.title: Cazuza Cultural Centre
carousel.slide10.desc: Show in Valença, in October 2025, featuring a special appearance by Tuca Mei.
p1.partnerships: Renowned Brazilian composer, poet and cultural producer, responsible for introducing names such as Simone and Clementina de Jesus.
p2.partnerships: A partner of Lemuriano, he is co-author of songs such as 'Dia Sim, Dia Não', recorded by Simone. The partnership bridges generations — with a 65-year difference — and Lemuriano regards Hermínio as one of his greatest artistic inspirations.
p3.partnerships: Singer and songwriter, artistic partner of Lemuriano. Together, they have developed work marked by deep songs and intense performances. They are known for their vocal harmony and for a rare affinity in lyrics and arrangements.
span.press_kit: A set of informational and promotional materials — texts, photos, samples and videos - for shows and events
h1.contact: Does your idea match my music? Let’s talk!
